titleStmt.title: Letter from Jonathan Pim to Augustine Birrell, 29 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Jonathan Pim

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter was written by Jonathan Ernest Pim (1858–1949). Pim was attorney general for Ireland (1914-1915). Following this he was nominated to serve on the Sankey Committee which adjudicated on internment in the aftermath of the 1916 Rising. The letter was addressed to Augustine Birrell (1850-1933), the chief secretary for Ireland. Birrell was appointed chief secretary in January 1907. In 1916 he resigned as he willingly accepted the political responsibility for the outbreak of the Rising in 1916. In the letter Pim mentions that John Robert O'Connell (1868-1943) served as his solicitor while Pim was attorney general for Ireland and recommends him for the vacant position of chief crown solicitor.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/7153/11

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
29.3.16 Dear Mr. Birrell. Sir John. R. O'Connell
informs me that he has
applied for the vacant post
of Chief Crown Solicitor for
Ireland. He was my Solicitor while
I was Attorney General, &
I therefore have had help with
both of his work, & of himself. He is a man of very considerable
ability & would the work of
the office (if it were given to
him) would not only be
safe in his hands, but also
Excellently done. I am
Yours Sincerely Jonathan Pim
Put with papers
7/4
